title: Buffalo Chicken Wings
subtitle: Crispy oven-baked wings tossed in buttery hot sauce
description: Buffalo wings are oven-baked or fried chicken wings coated in a simple, bracingly hot sauce of butter and hot sauce — nothing more. The original comes from Buffalo, New York, and the sauce formula is unchanged: melted butter combined with a Louisiana-style hot sauce in a roughly 1:1 ratio. The key to crisp wings without a deep fryer is a long bake at moderate heat followed by a short blast of high heat. The sauce goes on immediately before serving while the wings are still hot.
sourceNotes: Buffalo wings were created at the Anchor Bar in Buffalo, New York in 1964 by Teressa Bellissimo. The original recipe used Frank's RedHot sauce and butter, the combination that remains the standard. The dish is documented in the New York Times (1980) and is now one of the most widely known American dishes internationally.

recipeTools.notes: With a wire rack on top — elevating the wings allows hot air to circulate underneath and prevents steaming.
For tossing the wings in the sauce.

glossaryTerms.definition: A simple American sauce of melted butter combined with a Louisiana-style hot sauce (typically cayenne-based). The butter tames the heat slightly and gives the sauce its glossy, coating consistency.

ingredients.notes: Split at the joint into flats and drums — or buy pre-split
Not baking soda — this is what helps the skin crisp in the oven
A cayenne-based hot sauce — adjust to your heat preference
Optional — for a slightly sweet finish

steps: Pat the wings completely dry with kitchen paper — this is the single most important step for crisp skin. Any moisture on the surface will steam rather than crisp.
Toss the dry wings with the baking powder, salt, pepper, and garlic powder until evenly coated. The baking powder helps the skin dry out further in the oven.
Place a wire rack on a baking sheet. Arrange the wings on the rack in a single layer, not touching.
Bake at 180 °C fan for 30 minutes. Increase the oven to 220 °C fan and bake for a further 15–20 minutes until the wings are deeply golden and the skin is crisp.

While the wings are in the final blast, make the sauce: melt the butter in a small pan, add the hot sauce, garlic, and honey if using. Whisk briefly until combined. Taste — adjust heat with more hot sauce or tone down with more butter.
Transfer the hot wings to a large bowl. Pour the sauce over and toss immediately until every wing is coated. Serve at once with celery sticks and blue cheese dip or soured cream on the side.

content.text: Dry wings crisp; wet wings steam. Pat them as dry as possible with kitchen paper before anything else. The baking powder toss is the oven version of a technique that draws out surface moisture during cooking, producing a crispier skin than plain salt alone. Do not substitute bicarbonate of soda — it has a different effect.

Pat {{chicken-wings}} of wings completely dry. Toss with baking powder, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Arrange on a wire rack over a baking sheet. Bake at 180 °C fan for 30 minutes, then increase to 220 °C fan and bake for 15–20 minutes more until deeply golden and crisp.

immediately and serve with celery sticks and blue cheese dip or soured cream.
